title: Project Overview
description: MorningBriefing is a daily news briefing system that tracks NYT and WSJ articles with Chinese observer commentary. Start here to understand the project's purpose and structure.

title: Requirements & Architecture
description: Review the detailed requirements document that outlines the full architecture, including the three-observer analysis system, PDF management, and deployment considerations.

title: Web Server Entry Point
description: The Python HTTP server that serves the briefing website, mounting external PDF directories under /reports/ for browser access.

title: Main Website Pages
description: The main index.html provides today's NYT and WSJ links with three observer analysis sections. archive.html allows browsing past briefings by date.

title: Styling System
description: Dark-themed CSS stylesheet that defines the visual presentation of the briefing website, including observer analysis sections and PDF link formatting.

title: Daily Update Automation
description: The main automation script that generates daily NYT and WSJ briefing pages with observer commentary sections. This is the core content pipeline.

title: Combined HTML Builder
description: Builds a combined NYT + WSJ HTML page from the latest source files for consolidated reading.

title: Index Management
description: CLI tools for updating the reports index JSON and generating sample briefing content.

title: Data Layer
description: JSON indices that track all briefing entries by date and source, plus the observer configuration data.

title: Briefing Gallery
description: A gallery-style HTML page that displays all available briefing entries with visual navigation.
